channel.title: Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Foundry | May 2026
item.description: TL;DR Trace-based evaluation for external and hosted agents: Grade real production traces from Foundry, GCP, AWS, or any framework — no hand-curated datasets required. Grok 4.3: xAI's latest model lands in Foundry for advanced agentic and domain-specific workloads. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/whats-new-in-microsoft-foundry-may-2026

item.title: SharePoint Framework (SPFx) roadmap update – May 2026
item.description: May marks an important milestone in the SharePoint Framework journey and for the broader Microsoft 365 extensibility ecosystem. Most importantly, we reached general availability of SharePoint Framework 1.23, which represents a key step forward in our continued investment in a modern, stable, a Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/sharepoint-framework-spfx-roadmap-update-may-2026

item.title: Announcing AgilitySDK 721 Preview and an addition to LinAlg in Shader Model 6.10
item.description: Overview Today, we are pleased to announce Agility SDK 1.721-preview with new D3D features along with DXC 1.10.2605.4 adding VectorAccumulate to LinAlg in the existing Shader Model 6.10 preview. The previous 1.720-preview and SM 6.10 preview Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/directx/announcing-agilitysdk-721-preview-and-more-shader-model-6-10-features

item.title: Improve your agentic developer tools by grounding in Microsoft Learn
item.description: Development workflows span terminals, IDEs, background agents, and custom assistants. What matters is whether they draw from the same current source. Learn MCP Server gives any MCP-compatible agent direct access to current Microsoft documentation - one endpoint, noth Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/improve-your-agentic-developer-tools-by-grounding-in-microsoft-learn

item.title: PIX 2605.28-preview: previewing new PIX and D3D12 features
item.description: Today we released PIX version 2605.28-preview, which can be downloaded here. This release complements the new D3D12 Agility SDK that was released today, version 72 Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/pix/pix-2605-28-preview

item.title: How Azure Chaos Studio ships with hermetic Aspire end-to-end tests
item.description: End-to-end tests have a reputation problem. They're slow, they're flaky, and the moment you wire in a real cloud dependency, your CI builds become an exercise in waiting and retrying. Most teams I've talked to handle this one of two ways: they push everything down into integration tests and call it good (and lose confidence that the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/aspire/hermetic-aspire-tests-chaos-studio

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.122
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.122 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.122

item.title: SQL + AI, hands-on: Join a free workshop near you
item.description: If you work with Microsoft SQL regularly, the AI conversation right now probably feels a little exhausting. Every week brings a new platform, a new pattern, a new opinion about how you’re “supposed” to build AI apps. Most of it assumes you’ll start over. You don’t have to. We’re running SQL AI App in a Day workshops with Micr Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sql/sql-ai-hands-on-join-a-free-workshop-near-you

item.title: Verification-driven tooling prompts for fast-moving codebases
item.description: Introduction As software projects mature, internal tooling becomes critical for maintainability. By that point, the codebase has usually been in flight for months: multiple languages, real users, real deadlines, and the usual mix of conventions and shortcuts that accumulate when the priority is shipping. This is also the moment wh Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/verification-driven-tooling-prompts

item.title: I'm Starting a New Cosmos DB App. What Security Do I Actually Need?
item.description: You just created a Cosmos DB account. The portal handed you two keys and a connection string, it worked, and you moved on. That's what most developers do, and it causes problems later. This post is a guide for developers launching a new Cosmos DB app who want a secure default setup without enterprise-grade complexity. It focuses on the deci Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/im-starting-a-new-cosmos-db-app-what-security-do-i-actually-need

item.title: What's New for C++ Developers in Visual Studio 2026 (18.1 - 18.6)
item.description: Visual Studio 2026 versions 18.1 through 18.6 deliver a wave of improvements for C++ developers. For MSVC Build Tools, the v14.51 release is now generally available with runtime performance gains and improved C++23 conformance. We also introduced Sample Profile Guided Optimization to help you improve the runtime performance of your C/C++ progr Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/whats-new-for-c-developers-in-visual-studio-2026-18-1-18-6

item.title: How AI coding agents actually use your technology
item.description: You ship an SDK, a CLI, an API, and developers use it. Now AI coding agents use it too, except they use it differently than humans do. Most of the time you have no idea what's actually happening between "developer types a prompt" and "agent generates code with your technology." Is the agent reading your docs? Is it calling your MCP server? Is Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/how-ai-coding-agents-actually-use-your-technology

item.title: Introducing the Azure DocumentDB Blog
item.description: A dedicated home for MongoDB developers, MongoDB migrations, and modern document database development using Open Source on Azure Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/documentdb/introducing-azure-documentdb

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.122
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.122 (Insiders) Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.122

item.title: Deployment Operations, Requests and Queue
item.description: The PackageManager API submits requests to the deployment engine for processing. Each request represents an instruction for the deployment system to carry out a defined set of actions, collectively referred to as a deployment operation Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/insidemsix/deployment-operations-requests-and-queue

item.title: Sharing the result of a single Windows Runtime IAsyncOperation among multiple coroutines, part 1
item.description: Suppose you have a coroutine method called GetThingAsync(), and you want to do the work of getting "something" only once and caching the result. Here's the version that does no caching: struct Widget : WidgetT { IAsyncOperation GetThingAsync() { co_return co_await GetThin Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260527-00/?p=112361

item.title: Doing More with GitHub Copilot as a .NET Developer
item.description: For most .NET developers, the best way to get started with GitHub Copilot is not by learning every feature first. It is by using the right Copilot experience for the work already in front of you. Inline completions are part of that story, of course. They help with the everyday C# work that is repetitive but still necessary: filling i Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/doing-more-with-github-copilot

item.title: Give Your .NET MAUI Android Apps a Material 3 Makeover
item.description: Starting with .NET MAUI 10, you can opt your Android app into Material 3 (a.k.a. Material You) styling with a single MSBuild property. Material 3 is already live in a large set of controls today, more are on the way in upcoming service releases, and the plan is for it to become the default for Android in a future version. In this pos Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-maui-material-3

item.title: Visual Studio May Update – Plan, Review, Refine
item.description: There's a particular rhythm to good development work: you think, you try, you check, you adjust. This month's Visual Studio update leans into that rhythm. Whether you're sketching an approach with the Plan agent before t Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/visual-studio-may-update-plan-review-refine

item.title: If C# and JavaScript lets me await a Windows Runtime asynchronous operation more than once, why not C++/WinRT?
item.description: The Windows Runtime expresses asynchronous execution in the form of types like IAsync­Operation. Different languages choose to represent this concept in different ways. C# wraps it inside a System.Threading.Tasks.Task. JavaScript wraps it inside a Promise Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260526-00/?p=112354
